title: Voting system
abstract: In an embodiment a voting system is provided. The voting system comprises a supervisor unit. The supervisor unit is configured to manage the election via the voting unit and the printer. The supervisor unit determines a state of the supervisor unit, select one of a mock election and a real election based on successful authentication of at least one authentication card at a second card slot. The voting unit in communication with the supervisor unit is configured to receive a signal from the supervisor unit pertaining to initiating an election at the voting unit and transmitting to the supervisor unit, data pertaining to a plurality of votes cast at the voting unit. The printer prints a party symbol for each of the plurality of votes cast at the voting unit. The first microprocessor displays corresponding results at a display unit.
claims:
Claims Claims: 1. A voting system comprising: a supervisor unit comprising: a display unit; a first card slot adapted to receive a device card, the device card including information pertaining to a plurality of candidates contesting one or more elections; a second card slot adapted to receive at least one authentication card, the at least one authentication card including information required to be authenticated with information of the device card; a first microprocessor in communication with the first card slot and the second card slot, the first microprocessor configured to: determine a state of the supervisor unit; select one of a mock election and a real election based on successful authentication of the at least one authentication card at the second card slot; a voting unit in communication with the supervisor unit, comprising a second microprocessor, the second microprocessor configured to: receive a signal from the first microprocessor of the supervisor unit pertaining to initiating an election at the voting unit; transmitting to the supervisor unit, data pertaining to a plurality of votes cast at the voting unit; and a printer in communication with the supervisor unit, the printer configured to print a party symbol for each of the plurality of votes cast at the voting unit, 

 wherein the first microprocessor displays corresponding results on a display unit pertaining to the plurality of votes cast at the voting unit. 2. The voting system as claimed in claim 1 , wherein the state of the supervisor unit is determined as an active state in response to the device card being received at the first card slot, and the state is determined as an inactive state in response to the device card not being received at the first card slot. 3. The voting system as claimed in claim 1, wherein the first microprocessor selects the real election in response to the determination of the authentication card being received at the second card slot, and the first microprocessor selects the mock election in response to the determination that the authentication card is not received at the second card slot. 4. A method for providing a voting system, the method comprises the steps of: assembling a supervisor unit in communication with a voting unit and a printer; determining by a first microprocessor of the supervisor unit, a state of the supervisor unit; selecting by the first microprocessor, one of a mock election and a real election based on successful authentication of at least one authentication card at a second card slot of the supervisor unit; receiving by a second microprocessor of the voting unit, a signal from the first microprocessor pertaining to initiating an election; printing by the printer, a party symbol for each of a plurality of votes cast at the voting unit; transmitting by the second microprocessor, data pertaining to a plurality of votes cast at the voting unit; and displaying by the first microprocessor, results pertaining to the plurality of votes cast at a display unit.. 5. The method as claimed in claim 4, wherein the first microprocessor of the supervisor unit selects the real election in response to the determination of the 

 authentication card being received at the second card slot, and the first microprocessor selects the mock election in response to the determination that the authentication card is not received at the second card slot. 6. The method as claimed in claim 4, wherein the mock election executed by the first microprocessor includes the steps of: periodically checking for status of a start button, a close button, a count button and a ballot button; displaying on the display unit of the supervisor unit, results of the election in response to determining the status of the close button and the count button as pressed; checking if the ballot button is enabled and subsequently checking if the ballot button is pressed in response to determining the status of the close button as pressed; transmitting a signal to the second microprocessor to enable voting at the voting unit in response to determining the ballot button is pressed; receiving by the supervisor unit, the votes cast at the voting unit transmitted by the second microprocessor; printing each of the votes cast with the party symbol at the printer; and storing the plurality of votes cast at the device card. 7. The method as claimed in claim 4, wherein the real election executed by the first microprocessor includes the steps of: moving the device card to a locked state; receiving at the supervisor unit the votes cast at the voting unit and loaded onto the device card in response to the device card moved to the locked state; enabling a close button in response to authentication of the at least one authentication card, wherein the at least one authentication card is a first authentication card; 

 enabling a count button in response to authentication of the at least one authentication card, wherein the at least one authentication card is a second authentication card; and displaying results on the display unit in response to transmitting a signal to the device card to display the results. 8. The method as claimed in claim 4, wherein the authentication of the at least one authentication card by the device card utilizing the first microprocessor includes the steps of: generating a random challenge at the device card in response to receiving the device card at the first card slot; transmitting the random challenge from the device card to the at least one authentication card; transmitting a response generated by the at least one authentication card to the device card; verifying, the response generated by the at least one authentication card with the random challenge of the device card based on a key present at the device card, wherein the key is one of, a symmetric key, a secret key and a public key; and retrieving a pass from the device card in response to the verification of the response with the random challenge.
